@@ -0,0 +1,60 @@
+"""
+TelemetryStore — SQLite persistence layer for devflow task telemetry.
+"""
+from __future__ import annotations
+import sqlite3
+import threading
+from pathlib import Path
+
+_COLUMNS = [
+    "task_id", "timestamp", "task_category", "judge_verdict",
+    "probability_score", "impact_score", "oversight_level",
+]
+
+class TelemetryStore:
+    def __init__(self, db_path=None):
+        self._db_path = Path(db_path) if db_path else _DEFAULT_DB
+        self._lock = threading.Lock()
+        self._init_schema()
+
+    def record(self, payload: dict) -> None:
+        values = {col: payload.get(col) for col in _COLUMNS}
+        ...
+
+    def get_recent(self, n: int = 20) -> list[dict]:
+        ...
+

new file mode 100644
+++ b/hooks/tests/test_telemetry_store.py
@@ -0,0 +1,80 @@

+    def test_upsert_preserves_existing_non_null(self, tmp_path): ...
+class TestGetRecent:

spec: Add TelemetryStore SQLite persistence layer. Schema: task_executions with 31 columns covering agent behavior, quality signals, risk profile, harness signals. API: record() upsert, get_by_category(), get_recent(), get_failure_patterns(), get_context_anxiety_cases(), summary_stats(). Thread-safe via Lock. 14 tests.
harness_rules: File length limits: warn at 400, block at 600
existing_code: # telemetry/ directory did not exist before this commit. No prior implementation.
notes: New package added within its own boundary (telemetry/). Tests created alongside implementation. No imports from other devflow modules except stdlib. Clear single responsibility. Good pass case for a feature addition with full test coverage.
